calendar.matters:
Petition To Transfer Venue And Allow Compensation To Attorney
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susan Gibbs for Fremont Bank.

The Court asked counsel to explain why it should excuse the local rule. Ms. Gibbs stated that she filed a declaration, that it is for the efficiency to the Courts for this Court to hear the matter, that everything was done in this county, that they would have to wait an additional 2 years for it to be heard in Los Angeles, that the exchange traded funds took quite a bit of work in getting documents from everyone, that the Court granted the right to hold ETF's and that it was worth doing the work. The Court took the Petition to Transfer Venue and Allow Compensation to Attorney under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition To Transfer Venue And Allow Compensation To Attorney
Off Calendar Nov-02-2016 Continued To Nov-30-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Petition For Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition For Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susan Gibbs for (trustee) Fremont Bank.

The Court stated that this is the 2nd account and report, that it raises some issues such as venue, the issue of exchange traded funds and the local rules that require prior Court authorization for ETF's and the unexplained credit card charges. Ms. Gibbs responded regarding venue, that their 1st supplement agreed that there are different ways to interpret the code and that they will leave it up to the Court to decide, that their 3rd supplement agreed with the Court and they ask that venue be changed to Alameda County. The Court stated that a petition would need to be filed and set status on filing a petition for change of venue on October 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Gibbs stated that in regards to the investments, the examiner's notes cited 2574 regarding securities sold on an established exchange. The Court stated that the Local Rules spell it out, that it does not effect the Court's right to review for appropriateness and that the question is whether you feel you have adequately addressed why they should be approved. Ms. Gibbs asked that with the trust moving to Alameda County does the Court want the trustee to divest of the ETF's? The Court responded that it would have preferred to have more information on them, that it is inclined to allow those acts with the indication that a further accounting would need to address why the Court should allow it and that further ETF's would require Court authorization and explained that it wants to make sure with ETF's that the contents of the ETF are exchange traded. Ms. Gibbs stated that Fremont Bank uses Mainstreet Advisors to screen the investments, that in regards to the credit card issue there is an extensive email thread from the trustee to the family regarding receipts, that they do have receipts for most of the charges, that they all seem to be medical supplies, clothing and school supplies for Katelyn, that it has been a struggle to get the information but they are getting better at it and confirmed that the trustee is to reimburse the trust for the $192.00. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order) requesting that Ms. Gibbs submit a revised order which should include the status date for the petition to change venue, the reimbursement to the trust and the handling of the ETF's. The Court also stated that in the future courtesy copies are to be one sided only.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susan Gibbs for Katelyn Tam.

Ms. Gibbs explained that "collateral for credit cards" is a sub trust that Fremont Bank sets aside when they set up credit cards, that the credit cards are used for medical supplies, that the Bank reviews the purchases and that the credit cards have no cash withdrawal functions. Ms. Gibbs also stated that she was not sure if payment on the annuities are income over time. The Court stated that it is appropriate to increase compensation to the Parents. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
